agent_outputs.raw: The launch memo claim is not supported by the simulation evidence. The evidence shows improved simulator reward and 8-point immediate accuracy gain — short-horizon task metrics. End-of-term learning gains require delayed assessment evidence. The simulator may be unreliable when the new policy induces state distributions not covered by historical logging data. The memo language overstates the evidence. A randomized pilot is required. Critique wins.

Three issues. Immediate accuracy is not long-term learning. Simulator validity requires coverage of new-policy states which the team acknowledges is uncertain. Engagement, dropout, and teacher overrides affect end-of-term outcomes but are not measured. The memo claims certainty ('will improve') that cannot come from offline simulation. Critique wins.

The simulation results are promising but do not support the stated learning-gain claim. Immediate correctness in a simulator does not predict end-of-term assessment gains. The simulator's reliability for the new policy is uncertain. Engagement effects are unmonitored. The appropriate conclusion is: this policy merits a randomized classroom pilot. The current memo language goes further than the evidence supports. Critique wins.

synthesis: Unanimous critique_wins. All three agents converge on the same three issues. The simulator/immediate accuracy evidence is promising for justifying a pilot but not sufficient for the stated end-of-term learning claim.

etd_constraint.condition: Randomized classroom pilot measuring delayed post-test and end-of-unit assessment outcomes with dropout and teacher override tracking
etd_constraint.supports_critique_if: No significant delayed learning improvement, or engagement or dropout worsens under new policy
etd_constraint.supports_defense_if: Significant delayed learning gains with stable or improved engagement
etd_constraint.ambiguous_if: Some delayed assessment gains but differential effects across teacher override levels or student subgroups

all_issues_raised: Immediate accuracy not long-term learning (all agents)
Simulator reliability for new-policy states (all agents)
Engagement and teacher override effects absent (all agents)
